• Show All Code
  • Hide All Code

EOLIA Trial Supplementary Analyses

Overview

This supplement provides additional analyses for the primary analysis in EOLIA_BART_RD_RR.html. All preprocessing mirrors the primary analysis exactly so that results are directly comparable.

All treatment effects are reported on the risk difference (RD) scale: CATE(x) = P(Y=1 | Z=1, X=x) − P(Y=1 | Z=0, X=x), where Y=1 denotes death at day 60 and Z=1 denotes assignment to ECMO. A negative CATE indicates ECMO reduces mortality.

Analyses included:

  • Prior sensitivity across all four BART hyperparameters (k, power, base, ntree).

  • Comparator methods: Bayesian Causal Forest, Causal Forest, and XGBoost T-learner and S-learner.

  • Treatment effect heterogeneity by baseline risk:

    • Within-stratum risk differences across quartiles of predicted baseline mortality
    • BART-estimated CATEs conditioned on the prognostic score as a single covariate

Setup

library(BART)
library(dbarts)
library(caret)
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(tidyr)
library(purrr)
library(rpart)
library(rpart.plot)
library(gtsummary)
library(bcf)       
library(xgboost)  

source("clusterfunctions.R")

# Global seed
GLOBAL_SEED <- 60622
set.seed(GLOBAL_SEED)

Data Preprocessing

# Load the dataset
load("../../data/EOLIA_04122017/eolia_form.RData")

# Clean up variable types and remove rows with missing data
dat <- eolia[, c("age", "sexe", "ards_etiology_regroup3",
                 "pao2_fio2_rando", "paco2_rando",
                 "vasopressors_rando", "prone_rando", "manoeuvre_rando",
                 "steroid_rando", "sepsis_class_bin", "immunocomp_adm",
                 "lactate_rando", "rrt_rando", "sofa_rando_bin", "del_intub_rando_bin",
                 "compliance_rando", "driving_rando",
                 "ecmo_centers.f", 
                 "bras_en", "statut_vital_j60")]

addmargins(table(dat$bras_en, dat$statut_vital_j60))
##          
##             0   1 Sum
##   ECMO     80  44 124
##   Control  68  57 125
##   Sum     148 101 249
cat("Number of missing observations for each variables:\n")
## Number of missing observations for each variables:
sapply(dat, function(x) sum(is.na(x)))
##                    age                   sexe ards_etiology_regroup3 
##                      0                      0                      0 
##        pao2_fio2_rando            paco2_rando     vasopressors_rando 
##                      1                      1                      0 
##            prone_rando        manoeuvre_rando          steroid_rando 
##                      0                      0                      0 
##       sepsis_class_bin         immunocomp_adm          lactate_rando 
##                      0                      1                     13 
##              rrt_rando         sofa_rando_bin    del_intub_rando_bin 
##                      0                      0                      0 
##       compliance_rando          driving_rando         ecmo_centers.f 
##                     58                     58                      0 
##                bras_en       statut_vital_j60 
##                      0                      0
dat$compliance_rando <- dat$driving_rando <- NULL
dat <- dat[complete.cases(dat), ] # rows without missing values
dat$pao2_fio2_rando <- round(dat$pao2_fio2_rando)

cat("Number of observations in the analysis database: ", nrow(dat), "\n")
## Number of observations in the analysis database:  235
addmargins(table(dat$bras_en, dat$statut_vital_j60))
##          
##             0   1 Sum
##   ECMO     73  43 116
##   Control  64  55 119
##   Sum     137  98 235
# Impute NA values in a character column "adlmorelessf" with the mode
impute_mode <- function(column) {
  mode_value <- names(which.max(table(column, useNA = "no")))
  column[is.na(column)] <- mode_value
  return(column)
}

# Replace NA values with the median of each numerical column
impute_with_median <- function(x) {
  apply(x, 2, function(col) {
    col[is.na(col)] <- median(col, na.rm = TRUE)  
    return(col)
  })
}

# Standardize continuous covariates
contvar_unscaled <- dat[, c("age", "pao2_fio2_rando", "paco2_rando", "lactate_rando")]
dat$age <- (dat$age - mean(dat$age)) / sd(dat$age)
dat$pao2_fio2_rando <- (dat$pao2_fio2_rando - mean(dat$pao2_fio2_rando)) / sd(dat$pao2_fio2_rando)
dat$paco2_rando <- (dat$paco2_rando - mean(dat$paco2_rando)) / sd(dat$paco2_rando)
dat$lactate_rando <- (dat$lactate_rando - mean(dat$lactate_rando)) / sd(dat$lactate_rando)

# Treatment variable as logical
dat$bras_en <- ifelse(dat$bras_en == "ECMO", TRUE, FALSE)

# Rename covariates for easier use in decision tree plotting
colnames(dat) <- c("Age", "Sexe", "ARDS etiology", "PaO2/FiO2", "PaCO2", 
                        "Vasopressors", "Prone position", "Recruitment maneuvers",
                        "Corticosteroids", "Severe sepsis or septic shock",
                        "Immunocompromised", "Lactate", "Renal replacement therapy",
                        "SOFA score", "Interval between start on MV and randomization",
                        "Center",
                        "z", "dead60")

# Check the updated data structure
head(dat)
##          Age Sexe       ARDS etiology  PaO2/FiO2       PaCO2 Vasopressors

## 5 -0.8911945  Man               Other -0.1047021  0.46942049           No
## 7  0.2824977  Man               Other  0.9508372  0.33948667          Yes

## 1             No                    No              No
## 2             No                    No              No
## 3            Yes                    No              No

## 3                           Yes                No  0.9527511

## 7                           Yes               Yes  6.3909533

## 2                        No SOFA >= 11
## 3                        No SOFA >= 11
## 4                        No SOFA >= 11
## 5                       Yes  SOFA < 11
## 7                       Yes SOFA >= 11
##   Interval between start on MV and randomization       Center     z dead60
## 1                                        <3 days ECMO centers  TRUE      0
## 2                                        <3 days ECMO centers  TRUE      0
## 3                                        <3 days ECMO centers FALSE      1
## 4                                        <3 days ECMO centers FALSE      0
## 5                                      >= 3 days ECMO centers FALSE      0
## 7                                        <3 days ECMO centers  TRUE      1
# Set up variables for the model
z <- dat[, "z"]  # Binary treatment variable
y <- dat[, "dead60"]  # Outcome variable 
X <- dat[, !(names(dat) %in% c("z", "dead60"))]  # Covariates

# Reconstruct unscaled version of X for descriptive purposes
X_unscaled <- X
X_unscaled$Age <- contvar_unscaled$age
X_unscaled$`PaO2/FiO2` <- contvar_unscaled$pao2_fio2_rando
X_unscaled$PaCO2 <- contvar_unscaled$paco2_rando
X_unscaled$Lactate <- contvar_unscaled$lactate_rando

Sensitivity analysis of the prior specification

To assess whether the limited-HTE finding is robust to prior specification, we refit BART under a panel of prior configurations spanning under-regularized, default, CV-selected, and over-regularized regimes. The four hyperparameters varied are:

  • k (leaf-value shrinkage): primarily controls effect size magnitude. Larger k shrinks fitted values more tightly toward the overall mean.
  • power (depth penalty): primarily controls interaction capacity. Higher power penalizes deeper trees, making treatment-by-covariate interactions harder to learn.
  • base (split probability): also controls interaction capacity. Lower base reduces the probability of splitting, discouraging the deep trees needed for interaction detection.
  • ntree (ensemble size): more trees reduce each tree’s individual contribution.
# If the saved CV output is available, load the actual CV-selected
# hyperparameters rather than hardcoding them.

# best_hp <- which.min(orig_cv$CVMSE)
# cv_power <- orig_cv$Power[best_hp]
# cv_base  <- orig_cv$Base[best_hp]
# cv_ntree <- orig_cv$Ntrees[best_hp]

# If the saved CV output is not available, use the values reported in
# the primary analysis output. VERIFY these match before submitting.
cv_power <- 3
cv_base  <- 0.25
cv_ntree <- 400 

prior_grid <- tibble::tribble(
  ~label,             ~power, ~base, ~k, ~ntree,
  "default",               2, 0.95, 2, 200,     # BART default 
  "original_cv_selected", cv_power, cv_base, 2, cv_ntree, # CV-selected in primary analysis
  "under_regularized",     1, 0.95, 1, 50,      # everything relaxed: deep trees, few, weak k
  "deep_trees_default_k",  1, 0.95, 2, 200,     # isolates tree-depth effect (power=1 vs 2)
  "low_k_default_struct",  2, 0.95, 1, 200,     # isolates leaf-shrinkage effect (k=1 vs 2)
  "high_k_default_struct", 2, 0.95, 5, 200,     # strong leaf shrinkage
  "mildly_over_reg",       2, 0.75, 3, 200,
  "over_regularized",      3, 0.50, 3, 400
)

print(prior_grid)
## # A tibble: 8 × 5
##   label                 power  base     k ntree
##   <chr>                 <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl>
## 1 default                   2  0.95     2   200
## 2 original_cv_selected      3  0.25     2   400
## 3 under_regularized         1  0.95     1    50
## 4 deep_trees_default_k      1  0.95     2   200
## 5 low_k_default_struct      2  0.95     1   200
## 6 high_k_default_struct     2  0.95     5   200
## 7 mildly_over_reg           2  0.75     3   200
## 8 over_regularized          3  0.5      3   400

Helper: fit BART once with fixed priors, return full posterior

fit_bart_fixed <- function(X, y, z, power, base, k, ntree,
                           seed = GLOBAL_SEED, nchains = 4,
                           ndpost = 1000, nskip = 500, keepevery = 1) {

  x  <- cbind(z, X)
  x1 <- x; x1$z <- TRUE
  x0 <- x; x0$z <- FALSE

  set.seed(seed)
  bartmod1 <- lbart.cluster(
    x.train = x, y.train = y, x.test = x1,
    power = power, base = base, k = k, ntree = ntree,
    nchains = nchains, ndpost = ndpost, nskip = nskip, keepevery = keepevery
  )

  set.seed(seed + 1)
  bartmod0 <- lbart.cluster(
    x.train = x, y.train = y, x.test = x0,
    power = power, base = base, k = k, ntree = ntree,
    nchains = nchains, ndpost = ndpost, nskip = nskip, keepevery = keepevery
  )

  # Collapse chains: (ndraws * nchains) x n
  yhat1 <- apply(bartmod1$yhat.test, 2, rbind)
  yhat0 <- apply(bartmod0$yhat.test, 2, rbind)

  # Transform latent logit draws to probability draws
  p1 <- plogis(yhat1)  # P(Y=1 | Z=1, X)
  p0 <- plogis(yhat0)  # P(Y=1 | Z=0, X)

  # CATE on RD scale
  cate_post <- p1 - p0

  # Per-patient posterior summaries
  cate_mean <- colMeans(cate_post)
  cate_sd   <- apply(cate_post, 2, sd)
  cate_lo   <- apply(cate_post, 2, quantile, 0.025)
  cate_hi   <- apply(cate_post, 2, quantile, 0.975)

list(
    cate_mean = cate_mean,
    cate_sd   = cate_sd,
    cate_lo   = cate_lo,
    cate_hi   = cate_hi,
    cate_post = cate_post,
    p1        = p1,
    p0        = p0,
    config    = list(power = power, base = base, k = k, ntree = ntree)
  )
}

Run the prior-sensitivity grid

prior_results <- vector("list", nrow(prior_grid))
names(prior_results) <- prior_grid$label

for (i in seq_len(nrow(prior_grid))) {
  cfg <- prior_grid[i, ]
  cat("\n--- Fitting", cfg$label, "---\n")
  cat("  power =", cfg$power, "  base =", cfg$base,
      "  k =", cfg$k, "  ntree =", cfg$ntree, "\n")

  prior_results[[i]] <- fit_bart_fixed(
    X = X, y = y, z = z,
    power = cfg$power, base = cfg$base, k = cfg$k, ntree = cfg$ntree
  )
}
##

## [1] 5901 1155 3962 6046
##

##   power = 1   base = 0.95   k = 1   ntree = 50

## 
## --- Fitting over_regularized ---
##   power = 3   base = 0.5   k = 3   ntree = 400 
## [1]  744 4148 3964   18
## [1] 5901 1155 3962 6046
saveRDS(prior_results, "prior_results.rds")

Summary table across prior settings

prior_summary <- purrr::map_dfr(seq_along(prior_results), function(i) {
  r <- prior_results[[i]]
  tibble::tibble(
    label = names(prior_results)[i],
    power = r$config$power,
    base = r$config$base,
    k = r$config$k,
    ntree = r$config$ntree,
    mean_CATE = mean(r$cate_mean),
    median_CATE = median(r$cate_mean),
    sd_across_pts = sd(r$cate_mean),
    avg_within_post_sd = mean(r$cate_sd),
    shrinkage_ratio = sd(r$cate_mean) / mean(r$cate_sd),
  )
})

knitr::kable(prior_summary, digits = 3,
             caption = "CATE summaries across hyperparameter configurations")
CATE summaries across hyperparameter configurations
label power base k ntree mean_CATE median_CATE sd_across_pts avg_within_post_sd shrinkage_ratio
default 2 0.95 2 200 -0.079 -0.084 0.017 0.190 0.089
original_cv_selected 3 0.25 2 400 -0.046 -0.047 0.007 0.151 0.046
under_regularized 1 0.95 1 50 -0.090 -0.086 0.038 0.263 0.146
deep_trees_default_k 1 0.95 2 200 -0.077 -0.080 0.017 0.204 0.084
low_k_default_struct 2 0.95 1 200 -0.104 -0.110 0.031 0.232 0.135
high_k_default_struct 2 0.95 5 200 -0.034 -0.035 0.005 0.137 0.034
mildly_over_reg 2 0.75 3 200 -0.051 -0.053 0.007 0.160 0.045
over_regularized 3 0.50 3 400 -0.042 -0.044 0.005 0.146 0.036

The shrinkage ratio is defined as the SD of posterior-mean CATEs across patients divided by the average within-patient posterior SD. Values near 0 indicate the posterior cannot separate patients (heavy shrinkage); values above 1 indicate clear between-patient separation relative to posterior uncertainty.

CATE distributions across prior settings

plot_df <- purrr::map_dfr(seq_along(prior_results), function(i) {
  tibble::tibble(
    label = names(prior_results)[i],
    cate  = prior_results[[i]]$cate_mean
  )
})

ggplot(plot_df, aes(x = cate, color = label)) +
  geom_density(linewidth = 0.7) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dotted") +
  labs(
    title = "CATE distribution under different BART priors",
    subtitle = "Risk difference scale. Negative = treatment benefit.",
    x = "Per-patient posterior-mean CATE",
    y = "Density",
    color = "Prior configuration"
  ) +
  theme_bw()

ggsave("fig_prior_sensitivity_density.png", width = 9, height = 6, dpi = 300)

Caterpillar plots with individual credible intervals

key_labels <- prior_grid$label

cat_df <- purrr::map_dfr(key_labels, function(lab) {
  r <- prior_results[[lab]]
  tibble::tibble(
    label = lab,
    cate  = r$cate_mean,
    lo    = r$cate_lo,
    hi    = r$cate_hi
  ) %>%
    arrange(cate) %>%
    mutate(rank = row_number())
})

ggplot(cat_df, aes(x = rank, y = cate)) +
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = lo, ymax = hi), fill = "grey80", alpha = 0.6) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.6) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dotted", color = "red") +
  facet_wrap(~ label, ncol = 1, scales = "free_y") +
  labs(
    title = "Per-patient CATE posterior mean (line) and 95% credible interval (ribbon)",
    subtitle = "Risk difference scale. Negative = treatment benefit.",
    x = "Patient index (sorted by CATE within each panel)",
    y = "CATE with 95% credible interval"
  ) +
  theme_bw()

ggsave("fig_caterpillar_priors.png", width = 10, height = 9, dpi = 300)

Bayesian Causal Forest

Bayesian Causal Forest (Hahn, Murray, and Carvalho 2020) separates the prognostic surface from the treatment-effect surface using distinct tree ensembles.

# BCF requires a numeric covariate matrix: expand factors
X_mat <- model.matrix(~ . - 1, data = X)

# BCF requires a propensity score estimate (even in an RCT, we estimate it to capture minor imbalances)
pihat_glm <- fitted(glm(as.numeric(z) ~ ., data = as.data.frame(X_mat),
                        family = binomial))

set.seed(GLOBAL_SEED)
sink(tempfile())
bcf_fit <- bcf::bcf(
  y = y, z = as.numeric(z),
  x_control = X_mat, x_moderate = X_mat, pihat = pihat_glm,
  nburn = 2000, nsim = 2000, n_chains = 4,
  random_seed = GLOBAL_SEED, no_output = TRUE
)
sink()

mu_post  <- bcf_fit$mu    # (nsim * n_chains) x n, latent scale
tau_post <- bcf_fit$tau   # (nsim * n_chains) x n, latent scale

# Latent-to-risk-difference conversion. Under a probit link:
# P(Y=1 | Z, X) = Phi(mu + tau * Z)
# So CATE_RD = Phi(mu + tau) - Phi(mu)
cate_bcf_post <- pnorm(mu_post + tau_post) - pnorm(mu_post)

cate_bcf_mean <- colMeans(cate_bcf_post)
cate_bcf_sd   <- apply(cate_bcf_post, 2, sd)
cate_bcf_lo   <- apply(cate_bcf_post, 2, quantile, 0.025)
cate_bcf_hi   <- apply(cate_bcf_post, 2, quantile, 0.975)

cat("BCF (RD scale) summary:\n")
## BCF (RD scale) summary:
cat("mean of posterior-mean CATEs:", round(mean(cate_bcf_mean), 4), "\n")
## mean of posterior-mean CATEs: -0.0209
cat("SD across patients:", round(sd(cate_bcf_mean), 5), "\n")
## SD across patients: 0.00358
cat("mean within-patient post SD:", round(mean(cate_bcf_sd), 5), "\n")
## mean within-patient post SD: 0.03155
cat("shrinkage ratio:", round(sd(cate_bcf_mean) / mean(cate_bcf_sd), 3), "\n")
## shrinkage ratio: 0.113

XGBoost T-learner and S-learner (CV-tuned)

The T-learner fits separate outcome models on the treated and control arms and takes the per-patient difference as the CATE. Hyperparameters are selected via 5-fold cross-validation on log-loss with early stopping.

# Tune XGBoost via xgb.cv on log-loss with early stopping
tune_xgb <- function(X_train, y_train, nrounds_max = 300, seed = GLOBAL_SEED) {
  param_grid <- expand.grid(
    eta = c(0.01, 0.05),
    max_depth = c(1, 2, 3),
    subsample = c(0.6, 0.8), # Increased stochasticity to prevent overfitting
    min_child_weight = c(10, 20) # Prevents leaves with < 10-20 patients
  )
  
  dtrain <- xgb.DMatrix(data = as.matrix(X_train), label = y_train)
  best <- list(loss = Inf, params = NULL, nrounds = NULL)
  
  for (i in seq_len(nrow(param_grid))) {
    set.seed(seed + i)
    cv <- tryCatch(
      xgb.cv(
        data = dtrain,
        params = list(
          objective = "binary:logistic",
          eval_metric = "logloss",
          eta = param_grid$eta[i],
          max_depth = param_grid$max_depth[i],
          min_child_weight = param_grid$min_child_weight[i],
          subsample = param_grid$subsample[i]
        ),
        nrounds = nrounds_max,
        nfold = 5,
        early_stopping_rounds = 10,
        verbose = 0
      ),
      error = function(e) NULL
    )

    # Skip if CV failed or returned no valid iteration
    if (is.null(cv) || is.null(cv$best_iteration) || cv$best_iteration == 0) next

    best_iter <- cv$best_iteration
    best_loss <- cv$evaluation_log$test_logloss_mean[best_iter]

    # Skip if loss is NA or empty
    if (length(best_loss) == 0 || is.na(best_loss)) next

    if (best_loss < best$loss) {
      best <- list(loss    = best_loss,
                   params  = as.list(param_grid[i, ]),
                   nrounds = best_iter)
    }
  }

  if (is.null(best$params)) {
    warning("No valid XGBoost configuration found. Using fallback defaults.")
    best <- list(loss = NA, params = list(eta = 0.05, max_depth = 2,
                 min_child_weight = 5, subsample = 1.0), nrounds = 50)
  }
  best
}

# XGBoost requires a numeric design matrix: expand factors 
X_mat_xgb <- model.matrix(~ . - 1, data = X)

# Train on treated arm
set.seed(GLOBAL_SEED)
tune_t <- tune_xgb(X_mat_xgb[z, ], y[z])
set.seed(GLOBAL_SEED)
mod_t <- xgboost(
  data    = as.matrix(X_mat_xgb[z, ]), label = y[z],
  params  = c(list(objective   = "binary:logistic",
                   eval_metric = "logloss"), tune_t$params),
  nrounds = tune_t$nrounds, verbose = 0, nthread = 1
)

# Train on control arm
set.seed(GLOBAL_SEED)
tune_c <- tune_xgb(X_mat_xgb[!z, ], y[!z], seed = GLOBAL_SEED + 1)
set.seed(GLOBAL_SEED)
mod_c <- xgboost(
  data    = as.matrix(X_mat_xgb[!z, ]), label = y[!z],
  params  = c(list(objective   = "binary:logistic",
                   eval_metric = "logloss"), tune_c$params),
  nrounds = tune_c$nrounds, verbose = 0, nthread = 1
)

# Predict counterfactuals for the whole cohort
p1_xgb <- predict(mod_t, as.matrix(X_mat_xgb))
p0_xgb <- predict(mod_c, as.matrix(X_mat_xgb))
cate_xgb_T <- p1_xgb - p0_xgb # CATE (Risk Difference) scale, negative = benefit

cat("XGBoost T-learner (CV-tuned):\n")
## XGBoost T-learner (CV-tuned):
cat("Treated-arm tuning:", toString(sprintf("%s=%s",
    names(tune_t$params), tune_t$params)), "nrounds =", tune_t$nrounds, "\n")
## Treated-arm tuning: eta=0.05, max_depth=2, min_child_weight=5, subsample=1 nrounds = 50
cat("Control-arm tuning:", toString(sprintf("%s=%s",
    names(tune_c$params), tune_c$params)), "nrounds =", tune_c$nrounds, "\n")
## Control-arm tuning: eta=0.05, max_depth=2, min_child_weight=5, subsample=1 nrounds = 50
cat("Mean CATE:", round(mean(cate_xgb_T), 4), 
    "SD across patients:", round(sd(cate_xgb_T), 4), 
    "CATE range: [", round(min(cate_xgb_T), 4), ",", round(max(cate_xgb_T), 4), "]\n")
## Mean CATE: -0.0836 SD across patients: 0.5211 CATE range: [ -1.208 , 1.1161 ]

The S-learner fits a single outcome model on the full sample with treatment assignment included as a covariate, and estimates per-patient CATEs by toggling the treatment indicator while holding baseline covariates fixed.

# Numeric design matrix with Z as a covariate (same as BART S-learner)
X_mat_xgb <- model.matrix(~ . - 1, data = X)
X_all <- cbind(z = as.numeric(z), X_mat_xgb)

# Tuning function for S-learner
tune_xgb_s <- function(X_train, y_train, nrounds_max = 300, seed = GLOBAL_SEED) {
  param_grid <- expand.grid(
    eta = c(0.01, 0.05),
    max_depth = c(1, 2),
    min_child_weight = c(10, 20),
    subsample = c(0.6, 0.8)
  )
  dtrain <- xgb.DMatrix(data = as.matrix(X_train), label = y_train)

  best <- list(loss = Inf, params = NULL, nrounds = NULL)
  for (i in seq_len(nrow(param_grid))) {
    set.seed(seed + i)
    cv <- tryCatch(
      xgb.cv(
        data = dtrain,
        params  = list(
          objective = "binary:logistic",
          eval_metric = "logloss",
          eta = param_grid$eta[i],
          max_depth = param_grid$max_depth[i],
          min_child_weight = param_grid$min_child_weight[i],
          subsample = param_grid$subsample[i]
        ),
        nrounds = nrounds_max,
        nfold = 5,
        early_stopping_rounds = 10,
        verbose = 0
      ),
      error = function(e) NULL
    )

    if (is.null(cv) || is.null(cv$best_iteration) || cv$best_iteration == 0) next
    best_iter <- cv$best_iteration
    best_loss <- cv$evaluation_log$test_logloss_mean[best_iter]
    if (length(best_loss) == 0 || is.na(best_loss)) next

    if (best_loss < best$loss) {
      best <- list(loss = best_loss,
                   params = as.list(param_grid[i, ]),
                   nrounds = best_iter)
    }
  }

  if (is.null(best$params)) {
    warning("No valid XGBoost configuration found. Using fallback.")
    best <- list(loss = NA,
                 params = list(eta = 0.05, max_depth = 2,
                               min_child_weight = 10, subsample = 1.0),
                 nrounds = 50)
  }
  best
}

# Tune on full data
set.seed(GLOBAL_SEED)
tune_s <- tune_xgb_s(X_all, y)

cat("XGBoost S-learner tuning:\n")
## XGBoost S-learner tuning:
cat("  ", toString(sprintf("%s=%s", names(tune_s$params), tune_s$params)),
    "   nrounds =", tune_s$nrounds, "\n")
##    eta=0.05, max_depth=2, min_child_weight=10, subsample=1    nrounds = 50
# Fit final model
set.seed(GLOBAL_SEED)
mod_s <- xgboost(
  data    = as.matrix(X_all), label = y,
  params  = c(list(objective = "binary:logistic",
                   eval_metric = "logloss"), tune_s$params),
  nrounds = tune_s$nrounds, verbose = 0, nthread = 1
)

# Counterfactual predictions: toggle Z for everyone
X_treat <- X_all; X_treat[, "z"] <- 1
X_ctrl <- X_all; X_ctrl[, "z"]  <- 0
p1_xgb <- predict(mod_s, as.matrix(X_treat))
p0_xgb <- predict(mod_s, as.matrix(X_ctrl))
cate_xgb_S <- p1_xgb - p0_xgb  # RD scale, negative = benefit

cat("XGBoost S-learner (CV-tuned):\n")
## XGBoost S-learner (CV-tuned):
cat("Mean CATE:", round(mean(cate_xgb_S), 4),
    "SD:", round(sd(cate_xgb_S), 4),
    "range: [", round(min(cate_xgb_S), 4), ",",
    round(max(cate_xgb_S), 4), "]\n")
## Mean CATE: -0.0233 SD: 0.0664 range: [ -0.3853 , 0.2719 ]

Causal Forest (Generalized Random Forests)

Causal forests (Athey, Tibshirani, and Wager 2019) use honest sample splitting to prevent overfitting and provide asymptotically valid inference for individualized treatment effects.

library(grf)

X_mat_grf <- model.matrix(~ . - 1, data = X)

set.seed(GLOBAL_SEED)
cf <- causal_forest(
  X = X_mat_grf,
  Y = y,
  W = as.numeric(z),
  num.trees = 2000,
  honesty = TRUE,
  tune.parameters = "all",
  seed = GLOBAL_SEED
)

cate_grf <- predict(cf)$predictions
cate_grf_var <- predict(cf, estimate.variance = TRUE)$variance.estimates

cat("Causal Forest results:\n")
## Causal Forest results:
cat("Mean CATE:", round(mean(cate_grf), 4),
    "SD:", round(sd(cate_grf), 4),
    "range: [", round(min(cate_grf), 4), ",",
    round(max(cate_grf), 4), "]\n")
## Mean CATE: -0.0851 SD: 0.005 range: [ -0.0961 , -0.0722 ]
cat("ATE estimate:", round(average_treatment_effect(cf)[1], 4),
    "SE:", round(average_treatment_effect(cf)[2], 4), "\n")
## ATE estimate: -0.1022 SE: 0.0627

Cross-method CATE comparison

overlay_df <- bind_rows(
  tibble::tibble(method = "BART (default priors)",
                 cate   = prior_results[["default"]]$cate_mean),
  tibble::tibble(method = "BART (CV-selected)",
                 cate   = prior_results[["original_cv_selected"]]$cate_mean),
  tibble::tibble(method = "Bayesian Causal Forest",
                 cate   = cate_bcf_mean),
  tibble::tibble(method = "Causal Forest",
                 cate   = cate_grf),
  tibble::tibble(method = "XGBoost T-learner",
                 cate   = cate_xgb_T),
  tibble::tibble(method = "XGBoost S-learner",
                 cate   = cate_xgb_S)
)

# Violin plots
p_full <- ggplot(overlay_df, aes(x = method, y = cate, fill = method)) +
  geom_violin(alpha = 0.4, trim = FALSE) +
  geom_boxplot(width = 0.15, fill = "white", outlier.size = 0.8) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dotted", color = "red") +
  labs(title = "CATE distribution across methods",
       subtitle = "Risk difference scale. Negative = treatment benefit.",
       x = "",
       y = "Per-patient CATE") +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(legend.position = "none",
        axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 25, hjust = 1))
print(p_full)

ggsave("fig_cross_method_overlay.png", p_full,
       width = 9, height = 6, dpi = 300)
# Zoomed companion: visualize regularized methods
p_zoom <- p_full +
  coord_cartesian(ylim = c(-0.3, 0.3)) +
  labs(title = "CATE distribution across methods (zoomed to [-0.3, 0.3])",
       subtitle = "XGBoost T-learner distribution extends beyond axis limits.")
print(p_zoom)

# per-method summary table
method_summary <- overlay_df %>%
  group_by(method) %>%
  summarize(
    mean   = mean(cate),
    sd     = sd(cate),
    Q25    = quantile(cate, 0.25),
    median = median(cate),
    Q75    = quantile(cate, 0.75),
    range = paste0("[", round(min(cate), 4), ", ", round(max(cate), 4), "]"),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) 

knitr::kable(method_summary, digits = 4,
             caption = "Per-method CATE summary (risk difference scale)")
Per-method CATE summary (risk difference scale)
method mean sd Q25 median Q75 range
BART (CV-selected) -0.0458 0.0070 -0.0515 -0.0466 -0.0415 [-0.057, -0.0256]
BART (default priors) -0.0795 0.0170 -0.0925 -0.0843 -0.0684 [-0.11, -0.0253]
Bayesian Causal Forest -0.0209 0.0036 -0.0232 -0.0207 -0.0184 [-0.0321, -0.0133]
Causal Forest -0.0851 0.0050 -0.0889 -0.0845 -0.0811 [-0.0961, -0.0722]
XGBoost S-learner -0.0233 0.0664 -0.0302 -0.0068 0.0042 [-0.3853, 0.2719]
XGBoost T-learner -0.0836 0.5211 -0.4721 -0.1074 0.3239 [-1.208, 1.1161]

Treatment effect heterogeneity by baseline risk

We examine whether treatment effects vary across levels of predicted baseline mortality risk. The prognostic score is estimated from a logistic regression fit to the control arm only and applied to all patients. Two complementary analyses are conducted: (1) within-stratum risk differences across risk quartiles, and (2) BART-based CATE estimation using the prognostic score as the sole covariate, which asks whether the treatment-effect surface varies continuously with baseline risk.

Risk-stratified treatment effect analysis

Patients are stratified into quartiles of predicted baseline risk. Within each stratum, we report the risk difference (treatment minus control) with 95% bootstrap confidence intervals. No treatment-effect model is involved in this analysis.

# Fit a logistic regression on controls only to estimate baseline risk.
prog_dat  <- data.frame(y = y[!z], X[!z, ], check.names = FALSE)
prog_fit  <- glm(y ~ ., data = prog_dat, family = binomial)
risk_pred <- predict(prog_fit, newdata = X, type = "response")

# Stratify patients by quartile of predicted baseline risk
risk_q <- cut(risk_pred,
              breaks = quantile(risk_pred, probs = seq(0, 1, 0.25)),
              include.lowest = TRUE,
              labels = c("Q1 (Lowest Risk)", "Q2", "Q3", "Q4 (Highest Risk)"))

# Within-stratum unadjusted RD with bootstrap 95% CIs
B_strat <- 1000
strat_rows <- list()
for (q in levels(risk_q)) {
  idx <- which(risk_q == q)
  yq  <- y[idx]; zq <- z[idx]

  rd_q <- mean(yq[zq]) - mean(yq[!zq])

  set.seed(GLOBAL_SEED + which(levels(risk_q) == q))
  rd_boot <- replicate(B_strat, {
    bidx <- sample(idx, replace = TRUE)
    mean(y[bidx][z[bidx]]) - mean(y[bidx][!z[bidx]])
  })

  strat_rows[[q]] <- tibble::tibble(
    stratum = q,
    n = length(idx),
    n_treat = sum(zq),
    risk_diff = rd_q,
    CI_lower = quantile(rd_boot, 0.025, na.rm = TRUE),
    CI_upper = quantile(rd_boot, 0.975, na.rm = TRUE)
  )
}

strat_results <- bind_rows(strat_rows)
print(strat_results, width = Inf)
## # A tibble: 4 × 6
##   stratum               n n_treat risk_diff CI_lower CI_upper
##   <chr>             <int>   <int>     <dbl>    <dbl>    <dbl>
## 1 Q1 (Lowest Risk)     59      27    0.128   -0.0531   0.333 
## 2 Q2                   59      31   -0.0991  -0.332    0.143 
## 3 Q3                   58      23   -0.252   -0.498    0.0217
## 4 Q4 (Highest Risk)    59      35   -0.275   -0.484   -0.0586
ggplot(strat_results, aes(x = stratum, y = risk_diff)) +
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = CI_lower, ymax = CI_upper), width = 0.15) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dotted", color = "red") +
  labs(title = "Treatment effect by baseline risk quartile",
       subtitle = "Error bars represent 95% bootstrap confidence intervals",
       x = "Baseline Risk Quartile (Q1: Lowest, Q4: Highest)",
       y = "Risk Difference (Treatment - Control)") +
  theme_bw()

2x2 outcome tables by risk quartile

Raw outcome counts within each baseline risk stratum.

for (q in levels(risk_q)) {
  idx <- which(risk_q == q)
  yq  <- y[idx]
  zq  <- z[idx]

  tab <- table(
    Arm     = factor(ifelse(zq, "ECMO", "Control"),
                     levels = c("ECMO", "Control")),
    Outcome = factor(ifelse(yq == 1, "Died", "Survived"),
                     levels = c("Survived", "Died"))
  )
  tab <- addmargins(tab)

  ev_ecmo    <- sum(yq[zq])  / sum(zq)
  ev_control <- sum(yq[!zq]) / sum(!zq)
  rd_raw     <- ev_ecmo - ev_control

  cat("\n========== Risk quartile:", q, "(n =", length(idx), ") ==========\n")
  print(tab)
  cat(sprintf("  ECMO mortality:    %d/%d = %.3f\n",
              sum(yq[zq]),  sum(zq),  ev_ecmo))
  cat(sprintf("  Control mortality: %d/%d = %.3f\n",
              sum(yq[!zq]), sum(!zq), ev_control))
  cat(sprintf("  Unadjusted RD:     %.3f\n", rd_raw))
}
## 
## ========== Risk quartile: Q1 (Lowest Risk) (n = 59 ) ==========
##          Outcome
## Arm       Survived Died Sum
##   ECMO          21    6  27
##   Control       29    3  32
##   Sum           50    9  59
##   ECMO mortality:    6/27 = 0.222
##   Control mortality: 3/32 = 0.094
##   Unadjusted RD:     0.128
## 
## ========== Risk quartile: Q2 (n = 59 ) ==========
##          Outcome
## Arm       Survived Died Sum
##   ECMO          23    8  31
##   Control       18   10  28
##   Sum           41   18  59
##   ECMO mortality:    8/31 = 0.258
##   Control mortality: 10/28 = 0.357
##   Unadjusted RD:     -0.099
## 
## ========== Risk quartile: Q3 (n = 58 ) ==========
##          Outcome
## Arm       Survived Died Sum
##   ECMO          15    8  23
##   Control       14   21  35
##   Sum           29   29  58
##   ECMO mortality:    8/23 = 0.348
##   Control mortality: 21/35 = 0.600
##   Unadjusted RD:     -0.252
## 
## ========== Risk quartile: Q4 (Highest Risk) (n = 59 ) ==========
##          Outcome
## Arm       Survived Died Sum
##   ECMO          14   21  35
##   Control        3   21  24
##   Sum           17   42  59
##   ECMO mortality:    21/35 = 0.600
##   Control mortality: 21/24 = 0.875
##   Unadjusted RD:     -0.275

CATE as a function of baseline risk

We refit BART using the prognostic score as the sole covariate alongside treatment assignment. This asks whether the treatment-effect surface varies continuously with baseline risk, rather than testing at fixed quartile cutpoints.

res <- fit_bart_fixed(
  X = data.frame(prog_score = risk_pred),
  y = y, z = z,
  power = 2, base = 0.95, k = 2, ntree = 200
)
## [1]  744 4148 3964   18
## [1] 2982 2379 8349 1086

Refit BCF with the prognostic score as the only covariate.

X_prog_bcf <- matrix(risk_pred, ncol = 1)
colnames(X_prog_bcf) <- "prog_score"

pihat_prog <- fitted(glm(as.numeric(z) ~ risk_pred, family = binomial))

set.seed(GLOBAL_SEED)
sink(tempfile())
bcf_prog <- bcf::bcf(
  y          = y,
  z          = as.numeric(z),
  x_control  = X_prog_bcf,
  x_moderate = X_prog_bcf,
  pihat      = pihat_prog,
  nburn      = 2000, nsim = 2000, n_chains = 4,
  random_seed = GLOBAL_SEED, no_output = TRUE
)
sink()

mu_prog  <- bcf_prog$mu
tau_prog <- bcf_prog$tau
cate_bcf_prog_post <- pnorm(mu_prog + tau_prog) - pnorm(mu_prog)
cate_bcf_prog_mean <- colMeans(cate_bcf_prog_post)
cate_bcf_prog_lo   <- apply(cate_bcf_prog_post, 2, quantile, 0.025)
cate_bcf_prog_hi   <- apply(cate_bcf_prog_post, 2, quantile, 0.975)
risk_cate_df <- bind_rows(
  tibble::tibble(method = "BART",
                 prog_score = risk_pred,
                 cate = res$cate_mean,
                 lo = res$cate_lo, hi = res$cate_hi),
  tibble::tibble(method = "Bayesian Causal Forest",
                 prog_score = risk_pred,
                 cate = cate_bcf_prog_mean,
                 lo = cate_bcf_prog_lo, hi = cate_bcf_prog_hi)
)

p_risk_cate <- ggplot(risk_cate_df,
                      aes(x = prog_score, y = cate,
                          color = method, fill = method)) +
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = lo, ymax = hi), alpha = 0.15, color = NA) +
  geom_point(size = 1, alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_smooth(method = "loess", se = FALSE, linewidth = 0.8) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dotted", color = "red") +
  facet_wrap(~ method, nrow = 1) +
  labs(x = "Predicted baseline risk (prognostic score)",
       y = "CATE (Risk Difference)",
       title = "CATE as a function of baseline risk") +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(legend.position = "none")
print(p_risk_cate)

CART subgroup discovery by prognostic score

cart_fit_risk <- function(point_est, posterior, X,
                               maxdepth = 3, cp = 0.01,
                               metric = c("Risk Difference", "Risk Ratio"),
                               digits = 3,
                               main = "") {
  ## point_est  : length-n vector of per-patient posterior-mean CATEs (RD or RR)
  ## posterior  : (n_draws x n_patients) matrix of posterior draws (same scale)
  ## X           : data.frame of covariates for CART splits
  ## metric      : label for titles and printed output

  metric <- match.arg(metric)

  stopifnot(ncol(posterior) == length(point_est))
  stopifnot(nrow(X) == length(point_est))

  n_total <- length(point_est)

  data_cart <- data.frame(X, outcome = point_est, check.names = FALSE)
  cartmod <- rpart(
    outcome ~ ., data = data_cart, method = "anova",
    control = rpart.control(cp = cp, maxdepth = maxdepth),
    model = TRUE
  )

  ## Precompute posterior CrI for terminal nodes only
  frame <- cartmod$frame
  node_info <- vector("list", nrow(frame))

  for (i in seq_len(nrow(frame))) {
    if (frame$var[i] != "<leaf>") next
    idx <- which(cartmod$where == i)
    if (length(idx) == 0) next
    node_avg_draws <- rowMeans(posterior[, idx, drop = FALSE])
    node_info[[i]] <- list(
      mean = mean(node_avg_draws),
      lo   = quantile(node_avg_draws, 0.025),
      hi   = quantile(node_avg_draws, 0.975),
      n    = length(idx),
      pct  = round(100 * length(idx) / n_total)
    )
  }

  ## Custom node label: CrI only for leaves, default for internal nodes
  node_label <- function(x, labs, digits, varlen) {
    fr <- x$frame
    for (i in seq_along(labs)) {
      if (fr$var[i] == "<leaf>" && !is.null(node_info[[i]])) {
        info <- node_info[[i]]
        labs[i] <- sprintf("%s\n%d%%\n95%% CrI:\n(%s, %s)",
                           formatC(info$mean, format = "f", digits = 3),
                           info$pct,
                           formatC(info$lo,   format = "f", digits = 3),
                           formatC(info$hi,   format = "f", digits = 3))
      }
    }
    labs
  }

  ## Tree with CrI labels
  rpart.plot(cartmod,
             main = main,
             yesno = 2,
             node.fun = node_label)

  ## Printed summaries
  cat("\n====", toupper(metric), "SUBGROUPS ====\n")
  for (i in unique(cartmod$where)) {
    idx <- which(cartmod$where == i)
    node_avg_draws <- rowMeans(posterior[, idx, drop = FALSE])
    node_mean <- mean(node_avg_draws)
    node_ci   <- quantile(node_avg_draws, c(0.025, 0.975))
    cat("\nNode:", i, "  n =", length(idx), "\n")
    cat("  Mean", metric, ":", round(node_mean, digits), "\n")
    cat("  95% Posterior CrI for Subgroup Mean: [",
        round(node_ci[1], digits), ",", round(node_ci[2], digits), "]\n")
  }

  invisible(cartmod)
}
par(mfrow = c(1, 2))

cart_bart <- cart_fit_risk(
  point_est = res$cate_mean,
  posterior = res$cate_post,
  X         = data.frame(prog_score = risk_pred),
  main    = "BART"
)
## 
## ==== RISK DIFFERENCE SUBGROUPS ====
## 
## Node: 9   n = 68 
##   Mean Risk Difference : -0.023 
##   95% Posterior CrI for Subgroup Mean: [ -0.162 , 0.122 ]
## 
## Node: 6   n = 17 
##   Mean Risk Difference : -0.148 
##   95% Posterior CrI for Subgroup Mean: [ -0.407 , 0.123 ]
## 
## Node: 4   n = 88 
##   Mean Risk Difference : -0.21 
##   95% Posterior CrI for Subgroup Mean: [ -0.397 , -0.018 ]
## 
## Node: 8   n = 41 
##   Mean Risk Difference : -0.087 
##   95% Posterior CrI for Subgroup Mean: [ -0.286 , 0.107 ]
## 
## Node: 5   n = 21 
##   Mean Risk Difference : -0.165 
##   95% Posterior CrI for Subgroup Mean: [ -0.415 , 0.08 ]
cart_bcf <- cart_fit_risk(
  point_est = cate_bcf_prog_mean,
  posterior = cate_bcf_prog_post,
  X         = data.frame(prog_score = risk_pred),
  main    = "Bayesian Causal Forest"
)

## 
## ==== RISK DIFFERENCE SUBGROUPS ====
## 
## Node: 11   n = 29 
##   Mean Risk Difference : 0.014 
##   95% Posterior CrI for Subgroup Mean: [ -0.059 , 0.101 ]
## 
## Node: 6   n = 13 
##   Mean Risk Difference : -0.033 
##   95% Posterior CrI for Subgroup Mean: [ -0.094 , 0.018 ]
## 
## Node: 3   n = 59 
##   Mean Risk Difference : -0.059 
##   95% Posterior CrI for Subgroup Mean: [ -0.124 , 0 ]
## 
## Node: 8   n = 49 
##   Mean Risk Difference : -0.017 
##   95% Posterior CrI for Subgroup Mean: [ -0.077 , 0.038 ]
## 
## Node: 5   n = 54 
##   Mean Risk Difference : -0.046 
##   95% Posterior CrI for Subgroup Mean: [ -0.105 , 0.002 ]
## 
## Node: 10   n = 31 
##   Mean Risk Difference : 0.002 
##   95% Posterior CrI for Subgroup Mean: [ -0.062 , 0.073 ]

Baseline profiles by risk quartile

Comparing baseline covariate profiles across risk quartiles to describe the patients in each group.

# Using unscaled covariates for better interpretability
X_unscaled_with_risk <- X_unscaled %>%
  mutate(Risk_Quartile = risk_q)

X_unscaled_with_risk %>%
  tbl_summary(
    by = Risk_Quartile,
    statistic = list(all_continuous() ~ "{mean} ({sd})",
                     all_categorical() ~ "{n} ({p}%)"),
    digits = list(all_continuous() ~ 1)
  ) %>%
  add_p() %>%
  bold_labels()

Updated CART models with restricted depth

max depth = 1

par(mfrow = c(1, 2))

# BART
cart_bart_d1 <- cart_fit_risk(
  point_est = res$cate_mean,
  posterior = res$cate_post,
  X         = data.frame(prog_score = risk_pred),
  maxdepth  = 1,
  main      = "BART (Depth 1)"
)
## 
## ==== RISK DIFFERENCE SUBGROUPS ====
## 
## Node: 3   n = 109 
##   Mean Risk Difference : -0.047 
##   95% Posterior CrI for Subgroup Mean: [ -0.184 , 0.094 ]
## 
## Node: 2   n = 126 
##   Mean Risk Difference : -0.194 
##   95% Posterior CrI for Subgroup Mean: [ -0.359 , -0.024 ]
# BCF
cart_bcf_d1 <- cart_fit_risk(
  point_est = cate_bcf_prog_mean,
  posterior = cate_bcf_prog_post,
  X         = data.frame(prog_score = risk_pred),
  maxdepth  = 1,
  main      = "BCF (Depth 1)"
)

## 
## ==== RISK DIFFERENCE SUBGROUPS ====
## 
## Node: 3   n = 109 
##   Mean Risk Difference : -0.003 
##   95% Posterior CrI for Subgroup Mean: [ -0.061 , 0.055 ]
## 
## Node: 2   n = 126 
##   Mean Risk Difference : -0.051

max depth = 2

par(mfrow = c(1, 2))

# BART
cart_bart_d2 <- cart_fit_risk(
  point_est = res$cate_mean,
  posterior = res$cate_post,
  X         = data.frame(prog_score = risk_pred),
  maxdepth  = 2,
  main      = "BART (Depth 2)"
)

##

# BCF

  point_est = cate_bcf_prog_mean,
  posterior = cate_bcf_prog_post,
  X         = data.frame(prog_score = risk_pred),
  maxdepth  = 2,
  main      = "BCF (Depth 2)"
)
